description: Bounded cycle through a buffer. This fixture is not drawn from a spec example; it exercises the maxIterations event-lineage rule on an each node (a merge node on a cycle must not reset an iteration count). The cycle passes through a buffer (a merge node): step -> gate(buffer) -> unwrap -> step. Because the buffer preserves the operation's per-lineage count across the flush (element-wise max, here over a single-element batch), maxIterations bounds step to exactly 3 invocations, so the counter stops at n=3. If a merge reset the count instead, the cycle would never reach the bound and would not terminate, so this fixture pins the lineage safety rule.

fixtures.name: Bounded cycle through a buffer
fixtures.description: items.next increments n. step (an each node, maxIterations 3) feeds its output through a buffer with limit 1 (a merge node that flushes each event as a one-element array) and an unwrap transform back into itself, and also to output. step opens one invocation for n=0,1,2 (counts 1,2,3); the fourth arrival (n=3) exceeds maxIterations and is dropped, so the output is exactly [{n:1},{n:2},{n:3}]. The buffer carries step's count across the flush rather than resetting it, which is what keeps the cycle bounded.

graph.description: A cycle that routes an each node's output through a buffer and back into it, bounded by maxIterations (operation conduits cannot sit on cycles).
